skill.name: remotion
skill.description: Generate walkthrough videos from Stitch projects using Remotion with transitions, zooms, and text overlays.
skill.summary: Creates Remotion walkthrough videos from Stitch screen assets.
skill.author: google-labs-code

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak cryptography, path traversal, backtick execution, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives in documentation, examples, or static imports. The real concern is a Bash helper and workflow that download remote Stitch assets and write them to user selected paths.

medium_findings.title: Remote Asset Download Writes to User Provided Path
medium_findings.description: The helper script accepts a download URL and output path, creates the parent directory, and uses curl to write the remote response to disk. This matches the skill purpose, but it can overwrite files if an unsafe path is supplied and should require user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly uses user provided URL and path arguments for a network download and filesystem write. The behavior is intentional for asset retrieval, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Skill Requests Broad Bash and Web Fetch Capability
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash and web_fetch access and instructs agents to run npm, npx, Remotion, and curl commands. These commands are normal for video generation, but users should review commands before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The declared tools and documented commands are explicit and reproducible. No malicious command is present, but the permission scope is broader than a documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain descriptions, JSON fields, component props, or UI text. No cryptographic API, hashing function, or encryption logic is present at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static signal is weak because the matches are natural language and React props. Context review found no cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alert Is a Static Import
low_findings.description: The flagged ../screens.json path is a fixed TypeScript import. It is not constructed from user input and does not read arbitrary filesystem paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The location is a compile-time relative import. There is no dynamic path join, request parameter, or file read operation.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Alerts Are Documentation and Template Literals
low_findings.description: Most backticks appear in Markdown examples, checklist items, or JavaScript template literals used for styling. They do not create shell execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are prose, fenced examples, or a CSS transform template literal. The actual shell script uses explicit shell commands, which are reported separately.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Troubleshooting Text
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts map to guidance about readable text, transitions, MCP discovery, and documentation links. No probing or scanning behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are instructions, best practices, or public documentation links. No commands perform host, port, network, or environment discovery.

dangerous_patterns.title: Curl Download to Caller Selected Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The command pattern downloads a caller supplied URL and writes it to a caller supplied output path. This should be limited to intended asset directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell script shows direct assignment of arguments followed by curl output to the selected path. The risk depends on how the caller validates arguments.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast walkthrough videos for app screens, but manual editing is slow. This skill turns Stitch screens into Remotion compositions with transitions and overlays.
actual_capabilities: Find Stitch projects and screens through MCP tool calls.
Download Stitch screenshots and organize them as video assets.
Create screen manifests with timing, dimensions, and transition choices.
Generate Remotion React components for animated walkthroughs.
Guide preview and rendering through Remotion Studio, CLI, or MCP.
limitations: Requires access to Stitch and Remotion MCP servers or the Remotion CLI.
Does not create original design screens or repair missing assets.
Video quality depends on screenshot resolution and Remotion settings.
Voiceover support is guidance only and needs separate audio creation.

use_cases.title: Produce a product walkthrough
use_cases.description: Turn app screens into a short video that explains the main user flow.

use_cases.title: Prepare a design review demo
use_cases.description: Compile Stitch screens into a timed walkthrough for stakeholder feedback.

use_cases.title: Create a release highlight clip
use_cases.description: Render a feature video with screen titles, callouts, and transitions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a basic walkthrough
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Remotion walkthrough video for my Stitch project. Use fade transitions and show each screen title.

prompt_templates.title: Control timing and flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these Stitch screens in order, set each duration to 4 seconds, and add slide transitions between major steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add feature callouts
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract important labels from the Stitch screen data and add timed callouts for the main product features.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare final render outputs
prompt_templates.prompt: Preview the Remotion composition, fix timing issues, then render MP4 and WebM deliverables with clear output names.

output_examples.input: Create a walkthrough for the Calculator App project.
output: Identified the Stitch screens and planned the video sequence.
Prepared an asset and timing manifest for the walkthrough.
Outlined the Remotion composition, transitions, and preview steps.

output_examples.input: Make a release highlight video with callouts.
output: Mapped each feature to a screen and suggested callout timing.
Recommended slide and fade transitions for the user flow.
Provided render steps and expected output filenames.

output_examples.input: Check whether my video composition is ready to render.
output_examples.output: Reviewed assets, timing, dimensions, transitions, and final render readiness.

best_practices: Confirm download URLs and output paths before running asset scripts.
Keep manifest dimensions aligned with the actual screenshot files.
Preview in Remotion Studio before producing the final render.
anti_patterns: Do not write downloaded assets outside the intended project directory.
Do not mix screen sizes without explicit scaling rules.
Do not render final video before checking every transition and overlay.

faq.question: What inputs do I need to start?
faq.answer: You need a Stitch project name or ID and access to screen assets.

faq.question: Does it require the Remotion MCP server?
faq.answer: No. It can also guide rendering with the Remotion CLI.

faq.question: Can it download screenshots automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It can download screenshots when Stitch provides download URLs.

faq.question: Can I customize transitions and timing?
faq.answer: Yes. The screen manifest and composition settings control timing and effects.

faq.question: Does it generate voiceover audio?
faq.answer: No. It explains how to add narration, but audio creation is separate.

faq.question: What security review should users do?
faq.answer: Review network URLs and output paths before running download or render commands.
